target.name: Core Automation
target.founder: Jerry Tworek, Joanne Jang, Julia Villagra, Rohan Anil

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Core Automation, a stealth-stage AI & Software company founded by Jerry Tworek, Joanne Jang, Julia Villagra, and Rohan Anil, is targeting a Series raise of $500M–$1B at a $4–5B+ valuation. The comparable universe consists of 16 peers in the AI & Software sector, including 1 public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and 8 private companies such as Waymo, Figure AI, Scale AI, and Reflection AI.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The target lacks sufficient comparable datapoints at matching stage and valuation tier; a filtered peer set across same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier criteria yielded fewer than three datapoints, the minimum threshold for reliable median-based analysis. Constructing a median from the broader 16-company AI & Software cohort would introduce material noise given the wide dispersion in stage, business model, and capital intensity across the sector.

Without triangulation via comparable multiples, this valuation rests on the disclosed raise targets and stated range of $4–5B+. Key uncertainties include the actual revenue or profitability metrics underlying this range, the specific technical defensibility claims relative to named peers, and the stage-appropriate discount or premium warranted versus public AI software valuations. Further disclosure of operational metrics would be required to validate the raise valuation independently.
